query: There's a bug in our production billing service: safe_truncate() cuts mid-word instead of at the last newline before the limit. Fix it.
expected_behavior: Writes a regression test that reproduces the bug and is seen to FAIL red BEFORE the fix (iron-law TDD at Tier 2)
Then makes the minimum change to pass, refactors with the test green
Fixes the root cause, not just the one observed input; sweeps for sibling cases (no newline before the limit, newline exactly at the limit, limit >= len(text), empty text)
anti_behavior: Patches the code first and adds a test afterward (or no test)
Adds a test that asserts what the code now does without ever seeing it fail
source: references/engineering-workflow.md §3 (tier-aware iron-law TDD) + testing.md per-change-class contract
